name: I’m overwhelmed—what’s the very first thing I should do to optimize my website for answer engines and voice search?
description: Start by identifying the top 10 real questions your customers ask, then rewrite one or two key pages so each question is a heading followed by a 40–60 word dire

mainEntity.name: I’m overwhelmed—what’s the very first thing I should do to optimize my website for answer engines and voice search?

acceptedAnswer.text:
The best starting point is to identify the real questions your customers ask and rebuild a few core pages around clear Q&A structures.

You don’t need to redo your whole site at once; focus first on the pages most likely to earn voice answers.

**Key factors**
- Pull question queries from Search Console, support tickets, and sales calls.
- Add question-based H2s and 40–60 word direct answers near the top.
- Create an FAQ section with natural, conversational questions.
- Implement FAQ and Speakable schema on those pages.

Begin with a content audit of 5–10 priority pages, rework them into answer-first formats, then monitor changes in featured snippets, “People Also Ask,” and voice traffic.

Treat it as an iterative project: optimize, measure, refine, then expand to the rest of your site.
acceptedAnswer.description: Start by identifying the top 10 real questions your customers ask, then rewrite one or two key pages so each question is a heading followed by a 40–60 word direct answer. Add an FAQ section and FAQ schema, then expand to more pages once you see what starts to win snippets and voice answers.
